summary: ShaderEditor Bug Fixes

description.text:
Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/102642

The ShaderEditor had a few cases where it would not handle BuiltIn / Subresource Shaders correctly.

1. Upon re-launching, the ShaderEditor would not re-open BuiltIn Shaders, and throw an error (`ERROR: Resource file not found: res://new_canvas_item_material.tres::Shader_okjdl`)
2. Deleting the Parent Resource of a BuiltIn Shader would not remove it from the ShaderEditor
3. Selecting "Show in FileSystem" from the ShaderEditor would not show the Parent Resource of a BuiltIn Shader

To test these issues, the easiest way is to create a CanvasItemMaterial, and from the inspector Convert To Shader Material. This creates a ShaderMaterial with the CanvasItem Shader as a BuiltIn/SubResource, and from there you should be able to observe the incorrect behaviors.

This PR adds some extra case handling to address these issues. A few of the cases could behave incorrectly if legitimate files have File Names including "::", but I believe this is the case for most Godot SubResource handling?

summary: Baked VoxelGIData and LightmapGIData is still exported in dedicated server exports

description.text: - *Related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/109098 and https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12748.* ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.5.beta 2d113cc224cb9be07866d003819fcef2226a52ea ### System information Godot v4.5.beta (2d113cc22) - Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 575.64.03) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory ### Issue description Baked VoxelGIData and LightmapGIData is still exported in dedicated server exports. This unnecessarily increases the size of projects exported as dedicated servers, as these files can be pretty large in size when used for large levels. We can probably fix this by introducing PlaceholderVoxelGIData and PlaceholderLightmapGIData resources that inherit from VoxelGIData and LightmapGIData respectively, similar to PlaceholderTexture2D. These resources would only be there to allow the scene to load successfully (with no missing dependency errors), but would contain no actual GI data. ### Steps to reproduce - Export a ZIP pack of the project with the "Linux Server" export preset. - Open the ZIP archive and notice the VoxelGIData (`.VoxelGIData.res`) and LightmapGIData (`.lmbake`) is still there, and have the same file size with a standard Linux (non-server) export. Only the lightmap's `.exr` file is excluded. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [test_project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21518759/test_project.zip) Exported ZIPs for comparison: - [out_linux.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21518785/out_linux.zip) - [out_linux_server.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21518786/out_linux_server.zip)

summary: Shader sampler types sampler3D and sampler2DArray have only white default texture hints

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.5.beta3

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta3 - Windows 11 (build 22621) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8700K CPU @ 3.70GHz (12 threads) - 31.94 GiB memory

### Issue description

Trying to use a sampler3D texture with `hint_default_transparent`  does absolutely nothing to the alpha channel.

Looking at the code, you can see that they all fall back to a WHITE texture (except samplercubearray which falls back to black?)
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2a9ff392645f1e1e073d937f1f6fb8feac896b2f/servers/rendering/renderer_rd/storage_rd/material_storage.cpp#L1002-L1016

If these hint types are not supported by these sampler types, you'd expect them to throw an error at the shader compiler.

### Steps to reproduce

Make a ShaderMaterial, and add a uniform sampler3D with hint_type_transparent then don't set a texture in the inspector.
In the fragment shader, sample the texture and output the alpha channel to e.g. EMISSION
`EMISSION = vec3(texture(volume_texture, vec3(0.0f)).a);`
You will obviously see that the material lights up, even though the alpha channel is supposed to be 0

N/A.

summary: Cursor shape not being updated after end of window resize/move when using start_drag() or start_resize()

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6] and v4.5.beta3.mono.official [4d1f26e1f]
- Issue is reproducible in both C# and GDscript

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 22621) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen Threadripper 1950X 16-Core Processor

### Issue description

After resizing the window with ```get_window().start_resize()``` or moving it with ```get_window().start_drag()``` the cursor shape doesn't update as expected (Hovering over a Control node doesn't change the cursor shape to ```mouse_default_cursor_shape```).
The cursor shape begins updating correctly after a left mouse button input or after alt tabbing back to the window.

### Steps to reproduce

To reproduce this bug the game must not be embedded.

## get_window().start_drag()
- Add a Control node to represent the drag area. For the sake of visibility the MRP uses a Panel node
- Attach the following script
```gdscript 
extends Panel

func _gui_input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if (event is InputEventMouseButton):
		var mouse_event = event as InputEventMouseButton
		if (mouse_event.button_index == MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT && mouse_event.pressed):
			get_window().start_drag()
```
- To see the bug in action add a different Control node with the ```mouse_default_cursor_shape``` different from the Panel

### Video

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/99510244-715e-4b40-961d-6bbc35a1a8bc


## get_window().start_resize()
- Add a Control node to represent the resize area. For the sake of visibility the MRP uses a Panel node
- Attach the following script
```gdscript 
extends Panel

func _gui_input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if (event is InputEventMouseButton):
		var mouse_event = event as InputEventMouseButton
		if (mouse_event.button_index == MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT && mouse_event.pressed):
			get_window().start_drag()
```
- To see the bug in action add a different Control node with the ```mouse_default_cursor_shape``` different from the Panel

### Video

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/76838175-6106-4edf-a43e-bb5059fd528c


## Workaround
During testing, I found out that upon release of the left mouse button during the start_drag() or start_resize() there is no InputEvent for that action being called. This was verified with the following code.
```gdscript
func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if (event is InputEventMouseButton):
		var mouse_event = event as InputEventMouseButton
		if (mouse_event.button_index == MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT && mouse_event.pressed):
			print("Pressed")
		if (mouse_event.button_index == MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT && !mouse_event.pressed):
			print("Released")
```
The result after 3 consecutive drag operations being: 
```
Pressed
Pressed
Pressed
```

If the original code for dragging is changed to the following, the expected result is achieved.
```gdscript
func _gui_input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if (event is InputEventMouseButton):
		var mouse_event = event as InputEventMouseButton
		if (mouse_event.button_index == MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT && mouse_event.pressed):
			get_window().start_drag()
			var mb = InputEventMouseButton.new()
			mb.button_index = MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT
			mb.pressed = false
			Input.parse_input_event(mb)
```

Likewise the same fix works for the start_resize()
```gdscript
func _gui_input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if (event is InputEventMouseButton):
		var mouse_event = event as InputEventMouseButton
		if (mouse_event.button_index == MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT && mouse_event.pressed):
			get_window().start_resize(DisplayServer.WindowResizeEdge.WINDOW_EDGE_RIGHT)
			var mb = InputEventMouseButton.new()
			mb.button_index = MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT
			mb.pressed = false
			Input.parse_input_event(mb)
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

The Minimal reproduction project does not contain an example of the workaround described above.

[minimal-reproduction-project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21515132/minimal-reproduction-project.zip)

summary: Raycast2D unpredictable behaviour on edge of collisionshape without ignoring it

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible with example project in: 4.5.beta4, 4.5.beta3, 4.4.1.stable, 4.3.stable, 4.2.2.stable ### System information Godot v4.5.beta4 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7260) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads) - 63.88 GiB memory ### Issue description When using a Raycast2D, either with a `intersect_ray` from the `.get_world_2d().direct_space_state` or a Raycast2D node, when the raycast is not a child of a collisionshape and right on its edge facing outward, certain orientations make the raycast hit and some not. This ignores the `hit_from_inside` property. When offsetting the raycast slightly outside or within the object makes it not trigger, as well as rotation all the nodes together in certain orientations. This seems like some sort of floating point imprecision, especially when rotating manually. Strangely, when trying to rotate the parent Node2D with a script, the raycasts do not trigger at all. From testing, it seems like only the raycasts down and right are a problem, so the positive directions of the axis. (all raycasts in this image have `exclude_parent` enabled, and `hit_from_inside` disabled, the ones with a child label are children of the staticbody) I came across this when doing a pong like game and predicting bounces of the ball. When using the direct_space_state to raycast until collision, getting the normal and using that to "bounce" the raycast further only worked on the bottom wall. To solve it, I had to seperate out the two walls into seperate staticbodies to exclude each after a hit. It might be possible to offset the ray starting point a tiny bit, but you would have to make sure to not just move along the edge. But that is undesirable when trying to precisely raycast. The fact that further inside the body is "okay again", is also pretty unintuitive. According to a comment from 2021 in #55873, this might already be a known issue and maybe intended behaviour or too hard to fix. That was the only issue that I found actually adresses this issue, but I wanted to bring it up again since it feels very much not intended and also confusing when encountering it. tldr: a raycast might wrongly trigger right on the edge of a collisionshape in certain situations ### Steps to reproduce - create a parent `Node2D` - create a `StaticBody2D` - create a `CollisionShape2D` with a rectangular shape (e.g. (100,20) in size) - create a `RayCast2D` as a sibling to the `StaticBody` - set the `RayCast2D` right on a bottom or right edge of the `CollisionShape2D` (in this example on (0, 10) facing down) - in debug settings, set the Collisionshapes to visible - start the project and see the raycast change when manually rotating the parent `Node2D` ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [raycast-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21511151/raycast-test.zip)

summary: Using a LookAtModifier3D node and a BoneAttachment3D node (with OverridePose and External Skeleton enabled) inside another BoneAttachment3D , disables the LookAtModifier when pausing.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested versions
Godot v4.5 beta 4


### System information

Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6614) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900XT 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

Using a LookAtModifier3D Node in conjunction with a BoneAttachment3D Node (with override pose enabled and external skeleton) inside another BoneAttachment3D , disables LookAtModifier3D when pausing, even if the BoneAttachment3D is affecting a completly different bone to the LookAtModifier3D.

I tested in 4.4.1 and you can use this set up just fine without any issues.

I also don't think its just "disabling it on pause", Its making any collider attached to the lookat bone behave in a strange way, as if it was in 2 positions at once. the neutral position and the position after the lookat is applied, but this the best way I can decribe whats happening

this is what it looks like now in 4.5 beta 4

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/83100d6e-3fcc-4e90-a4fa-efbf225318f6

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0f45ec28-e306-4d1d-8908-ab19664d0bd6

summary: No OpenXR action sets when exporting with "Shader Baker" enabled

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in Godot 4.5 beta 3 and 5. 
Not reproducible in Godot 4.4

### System information

Quest 3, Valve Index

### Issue description

Creating a windows release build and playing it with a non-default OpenXR_Action_Set will create per frame error saying action_sets are empty.
I say non-default because im still trying to figure out what the fuck bugs the bastard out, Just saving the default OpenXR_Action_Set creates multiple OpenXR errors. Im concerned it might be something to do with the saving system? It may also be a data issue, seeing as any notable change in the default creates issues.
This error happens with Quest Link and SteamVR. 



This error does not happen in-editor. 
Notably SteamVR refuses to even think about creating Controller Bindings, but, i mean, thats kinda the issue with not having action sets.
```
OpenXR: failed to attach action sets! [ XR_ERROR_VALIDATION_FAILURE ]
OpenXR: Configuring interface
OpenXR: Session begun

ERROR: Condition "active_sets.is_empty()" is true. Returning: false
   at: sync_action_sets (modules/openxr/openxr_api.cpp:3463)
ERROR: Condition "active_sets.is_empty()" is true. Returning: false
   at: sync_action_sets (modules/openxr/openxr_api.cpp:3463)
ERROR: Condition "active_sets.is_empty()" is true. Returning: false
   at: sync_action_sets (modules/openxr/openxr_api.cpp:3463)
[Continues for runtime]
```

### Steps to reproduce

- Create or Port a Vr Project
- Create and save unique OpenXr_Action_set
- Build Debug Project
- Play with Console

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I mean, Its a action_set error, just not having it means controllers wont track and your console fills with blood. so its basically just checking the log after building. Heres the project I've been using trying to debug it. 
It's happened in my main project and the MRP below

summary: Unify Script Editor color pickers

description.text: - Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/89354 - Addresses (partially) https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/12899 The "Pick Color" context menu option now shares logic with the inline colors. You can now see the option in the context menu if you right click outside of `Color`, as long as it is part of the color definition. Added color constant support since the Pick Color option supported them. They don't have their own mode in the options, so it will use rgb when changing the color. Moved the language specific color parsing and text creation to gdscript language. Also removed the `goto_option` bool for the context menu, it was always false.

description.text:
### Tested versions

### System information

 Godot v4.5.beta (2d113cc22) - Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 575.64.03) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory

### Issue description

When running with a [build with sanitizers enabled](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/contributing/development/debugging/using_sanitizers.html), I get this error when a WAV file is imported as QOA:

```
thirdparty/misc/qoa.h:296:13: runtime error: signed integer overflow: 34725 * 65536 cannot be represented in type 'int'
```

(Note that this error is not visible in standard builds.)

cc @DeeJayLSP

- Import a WAV file as QOA (the default).

summary: Shader baker still exports a shader cache for dedicated server exports

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.beta 2d113cc224cb9be07866d003819fcef2226a52ea

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta (2d113cc22) - Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 575.64.03) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory

### Issue description

The [shader baker](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/102552) still exports a shader cache for dedicated server exports, even though these are always running in headless mode and will therefore never use the cached shaders. This makes the PCK/ZIP size unnecessarily larger and makes the export slower to complete.

summary: Translation.get_message() only works for first loaded translation resource.

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3 stable (possibly earlier) to 4.4.1 stable (possibly later)

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - GeForce GT 740 - AMD FX(tm)-8300 Eight-Core Processor (8 threads)

### Issue description

I wanted to have a special feature in my game where if the game is using a translation, the player would have an option to toggle whether they would want the name of the characters to be translated.

To implement such feature, I made a function that checks for a setting (ie `use_translated_names`) to decide whether to return a translated string using the `tr()` function or returning the string from the english translation using `TranslationServer.get_translation_object("en").get_message(key)`

However, due to the way I organized the translation resources on the `Translation` tab of `ProjectSettings> Localization`, I noticed that for some reason, `TranslationServer.get_translation_object("en").get_message(key)` only returns a string that exists in the first file on the translation resources list on the `Translation` tab of `ProjectSettings> Localization`, otherwise it returns an empty string.

In fact, consider swapping the order of the files in the `ProjectSettings> Localization > Translations` tab and you will see the other sentence on the screen will translate and the other one won't.

I would like to know what exactly is the intended usage of `Translation.get_message()` and why it can only be used on keys from the first file in the list.

### Steps to reproduce

**Using the minimal reproduction project:**
- Toggle the 2 Checkboxes, each translate one of two text labels on the screen using the `tr()` or the `TranslationServer.get_translation_object("en").get_message(key)` function, one of the labels will be invisible when translated using `TranslationServer.get_translation_object("en").get_message(key)`
- Swap around the order of the translation files loaded in `ProjectSettings> Localization > Translations`
- Now, see the other label become invisible when the checkboxes are ticked, while the other is invisible.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

3.Fold and unfold the library.

### Issue description

A `CodeEdit` is giving errors when any gutter icon (breakpoint, bookmark, executing line) is drawn for the first time when running the project in the Game view.

Error: `GLES3::TextureStorage::texture_set_size_override: Condition "p_width <= 0 || p_width > 16384" is true.`

The error doesn't happen in exported projects; only when running the project from the editor in the Game view with specific project settings.


### Steps to reproduce

Settings:
- Render mode is `Compatibility`.
- Main window size is set to 1920x1080, but I added `editor_hint` overrides for `Window Width Override` and `Window Height Override`, setting it to 1280x720.
- Stretch mode is `canvas_items` and stretch aspect is `expand`.
- Enabled `Embed Game on Next Play` in the Game tab.

These settings ensure the floating Game window starts at 1280x720, although the project's main resolution is 1920x1080; but when `Embed Game on Next Play` is disabled, the project starts in 1920x1080 instead.

- Add a `CodeEdit` and enable `gutters_draw_breakpoints_gutter`, `gutters_draw_bookmarks` and `gutters_draw_executing_lines`
- Mark lines to draw the icons in the gutter.

```gdscript
extends CodeEdit

func _ready() -> void:
	text = """
mov r 5
add r 2
LOOP:
    show r
    dec r
    eq r 4
    jne LOOP
halt
	"""
	
	set_line_as_breakpoint(2, true)
	set_line_as_bookmarked(3, true)
	set_line_as_executing(4, true)

```

summary: Colorpicker slider/spinbox yields incorrect values in rgb mode

description.text:
### Tested versions

Master(https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/0dd9178269e447aca805bc4b4a9a840119c10275) (Couple days old)

### System information

-

### Issue description

Was thinking a bit more about https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/109012. How come `11.0/255.0` gets saved as `0.04313725` in the scene file when there are two closer decimal representations? Well it seems the fault lies with the color picker sliders (doesn't invalidate the pr - rounding is still the correct approach as `10.9999983943998813629150390625` should still definitely result in choosing 11 not 10).

When setting a slider to an integer value in RGB mode, the picked color float value is not the closest presentable float value. E.g. Color #0b0000, red channel:
```
0.04313725490196078371507582005506 - double, 11 / 255
0.0431372560560703277587890625 - Color.html(#0b0000)
0.0431372560560703277587890625 - Color8(11, 11, 11)
0.0431372560560703277587890625 - Color picker, html #0b0000
0.0431372486054897308349609375 - Color picker, slider 11 (2 bits off from closest possible)
```
Why important? Multiplying and flooring yields different values:
```
0.0431372560560703277587890625 * 255.0 = 11.0000002942979335784912109375
0.0431372486054897308349609375 * 255.0 = 10.9999983943998813629150390625
```



### Steps to reproduce

1. Choose a value in color picker using sliders in RGB mode
2. Compare the value to setting it using html.
3. Observe that the values don't match.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[picker-underreperesented.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21477412/picker-underreperesented.zip)

summary: Add inspector Locking System

description.text:
This pull request introduces a new Inspector Locking System to the Godot Editor. It enables developers to lock the Inspector to a specific node, allowing them to interact with other nodes or perform operations in the scene without losing reference to the previously inspected node's properties.

All additions have been clearly marked in the ```editor/inspector/editor_inspector.cpp``` using the following format:
```CPP
// <Code Title>
...
// End
```

Features Implemented

1. Inspector Lock Button
      -  A new toggle button has been added to the Inspector panel.
      -  Once enabled, it locks the Inspector to the currently selected node.

2. Dynamic Icon Feedback
      -  The button changes its icon color to green when locked and white when unlocked.
      -  This provides immediate visual feedback to the user.

3. Property Editing in Lock State
      -  While locked, the Inspector continues to reflect and apply property changes to the locked node, even if the user selects or
interacts with other nodes in the scene.

4. Safe Cleanup on Node Deletion
     -  If the locked node is deleted, the system automatically unlocks the Inspector and clears the stale reference, preventing memory leaks and engine freezes.

5. Non-intrusive Scene Interactions
      -  Interacting with other nodes (e.g., changing transforms, deleting them, etc.) does not affect the state of the locked Inspector or the locked node.

6. Graceful Unlocking
      -  Upon unlocking, the Inspector immediately reflects the currently selected node’s properties, resuming normal behavior seamlessly.
      


Testing:-
1. The feature was built and tested in a local fork of Godot's GDScript version (Haven't tried with the mono version yet).
2. Multiple edge cases were accounted for.
3. No memory leaks, crashes, or freezes observed after the latest patch.

For the demo video of the feature, check this out: https://youtu.be/NV9M_Ofev1A

summary: [macOS] Game crashes when freeing a control under the cursor

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in both 4.4.stable and 4.1.stable (I didn't test any other)

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.3.2) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M4 (Apple9) - Apple M4 (10 threads)

I tested and **cannot reproduce** on Windows:
Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7680) - AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

The Godot game (not the editor) crashes when freeing a Control node that was just presented under the mouse cursor.

### Steps to reproduce

- Add a control in a scene (e.g. a `ColorRect`)
- Listen to the `mouse_entered` and `mouse_exited` signals
- On mouse entered, present a new control on top of the current one (as if it were a tooltip)
- On mouse exited, remove and `queue_free` that makeshift "tooltip"
- Moving your mouse around the main control shows the tooltip, then immediately mouse_exited is called which hides and free the tooltip, and mouse entered is called again in a loop. The game then crashes

The game doesn't crash if you don't call `queue_free` on that "tooltip" control, but it's never deleted from memory (tested by attaching a script to it and logging `NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE`).

The game also doesn't crash if presenting a regular Node2D, or setting the control's mouse filter to `MOUSE_FILTER_IGNORE`.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[godot-mouseover-crash.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21473697/godot-mouseover-crash.zip)

The code to reproduce is very simple, just attach this to any control node in a scene:
```gdscript
extends ColorRect

var currentPopup: Control = null

func _ready() -> void:
	mouse_entered.connect(Callable(self, "on_mouse_enter"))
	mouse_exited.connect(Callable(self, "on_mouse_exit"))

func on_mouse_enter():
	currentPopup = ColorRect.new()
	currentPopup.color = Color.BLUE
	currentPopup.size = Vector2(120, 80)
	get_parent().add_child(currentPopup)
	currentPopup.set_position(self.get_screen_position())

func on_mouse_exit():
	if currentPopup != null:
		get_parent().remove_child(currentPopup)
		currentPopup.queue_free()
		currentPopup = null
```
